name: Jafari No-Interest
description_short: Faith-based credit union in Texas offering no-interest loans and savings accounts to members of Jafari community centers, funded by member fees and donations rather than interest.
description_long:
Jafari No-Interest Credit Union (JNICU) was established in early 2016 in Houston, Texas, after receiving approval from the Texas Credit Union Department and the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA). The organization was created by and serves the Jafari community, operating on Islamic principles that prohibit usury (riba) as taught in the Holy Quran. Like traditional credit unions, JNICU is a not-for-profit institution where member deposits are federally insured by NCUA up to $250,000.

JNICU offers no-interest savings accounts and no-interest loans ranging from $1,200 to $35,000, including refinance options for auto, student, and home loans; personal loans; home/car repair loans; medical loans; college loans; and business equipment loans. The credit union maintains low operating expenses of approximately $40,000 annually by using donated office space and unpaid volunteer staff. Operating costs are covered through member account fees (ranging from $3–$60 monthly depending on loan-to-savings ratio), voluntary donations, and application fees ($25–$50 per loan).

What distinguishes JNICU is its explicit religious mission grounded in Islamic finance principles and its elimination of interest charges entirely—members who borrow $8,000 avoid interest that might otherwise cost thousands of dollars. The organization operates transparently with published quarterly financial reports on the NCUA website. As of December 2025, JNICU has 365 members, $2.1 million in deposits, $3.6 million in total loans issued since inception, and members have collectively avoided $1.155 million in interest payments.

However, JNICU has significant limitations. Membership is restricted to attendees of specific Jafari community centers in Houston, Dallas, and Austin (though family members nationwide can join if related to existing members). The credit union accepts deposits and processes transactions via ACH only—no cash, no ATM access, no debit/credit cards, and no checking accounts. Borrowers must have made at least three monthly payments on existing loans to refinance. With only 365 members and $1.155 million in deposits, the organization is very small and operates on a volunteer model, raising questions about sustainability and service reliability. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: No-interest savings accounts with NCUA insurance up to $250,000
Auto loan refinancing up to $35,000 at no interest
Student loan refinancing up to $35,000 at no interest
Home loan refinancing up to $35,000 at no interest
Home/car repair loans $1,200–$8,000 at no interest
College tuition loans $1,200–$8,000 at no interest
Business equipment purchase/repair loans $5,000–$16,000 at no interest
Membership for Jafari community center attendees and their family members
Quarterly financial reporting and NCUA oversight
pros: Completely no-interest loans eliminate interest charges—members have avoided $1.155 million in interest since inception
Federally insured deposits up to $250,000 through NCUA, providing same protection as traditional banks
Transparent operations with published quarterly financial reports accessible on NCUA website
Low monthly account fees ($3–$60) compared to traditional bank charges, with $3 minimum for members with no loans
Multiple loan types available including auto/student/home refinance (up to $35,000), personal, medical, college, and business equipment loans
Faith-based mission aligned with Islamic finance principles appeals to religiously observant members
Operates as not-for-profit member-owned cooperative rather than profit-driven institution
cons: Severely restricted membership—limited to Jafari community center attendees in Houston, Dallas, Austin (plus family members nationwide)
No cash handling, ATM access, debit/credit cards, or checking accounts; deposits/withdrawals by ACH only
Very small institution (365 members, $2.1M deposits) raises sustainability concerns and limits service infrastructure
Non-refundable loan application fees ($25–$50) on top of monthly account fees
Must have made at least 3 payments on existing loans to qualify for refinancing
best_for: Members of Jafari community centers in Texas seeking interest-free loans aligned with Islamic principles
Devout Muslims nationwide with family connections to existing JNICU members who want to avoid interest
Borrowers refinancing auto or student loans who qualify and want to eliminate interest payments
Community members seeking savings accounts with federal insurance and lower fees than commercial banks
diagnosis: JNICU is best for devout Muslims with access to Jafari community centers in Texas or existing family connections to members who want to eliminate interest charges on loans and align borrowing with Islamic principles. The critical caveat is that membership is geographically restricted and the institution is very small with minimal infrastructure—this is a niche, volunteer-driven credit union suitable only for those within its specific community and willing to conduct banking entirely through ACH transfers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
